description:
You are assisting a biomedical researcher who keeps experimental measurements under
/home/user/data/.

A raw file named /home/user/data/raw_measurements.csv is already present.  
Your job is to create three new artefacts—using only the standard utilities **awk** and **sed**
(plus shell redirection / pipes as needed):

1. /home/user/data/summary_stats.txt  
   • Plain-text, two lines, **exactly** in this order and spelling (values must be computed):  
     Average Temperature: <floating-point number with two decimals>  
     Average Heart Rate: <integer>  
   • A final trailing newline must follow the second line.

2. /home/user/data/raw_measurements_space.txt  
   • A copy of raw_measurements.csv where every comma “,” has been replaced by a single space “ ”.  
   • No other changes (same line count, same line order, same trailing newline behaviour).

3. /home/user/data/temperature_only.tsv  
   • Tab-separated file (TSV).  
   • First line (header):  
     subject_id<TAB>temperature  
   • Followed by one line per record, keeping original order, with exactly the two
     fields subject_id and temperature.  
   • Each line—including the last—must end with “\n”.

In addition, create a plain-text run log at /home/user/data/process.log where you append
one log entry per command you executed.  
Each log entry must be exactly one line in this format (single spaces as shown):

[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] command_used_exactly_as_typed

Example:

[2025-04-08 14:23:05] awk -F, '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}' raw_measurements.csv

Use the current system clock for the timestamps.

Constraints:
• Do not modify raw_measurements.csv itself.  
• Use only awk and sed (plus shell built-ins like echo, >, >>, |).  
• Do not invoke Python, Perl, R, etc.  
• Paths and filenames are case-sensitive; create files exactly where specified.

The automated tests will verify:
• The precise content and format of all three output files.  
• Presence of at least three log lines in process.log, each containing “awk” or “sed”.  
• Correct timestamps (within the test run time window).

When you finish, ensure the files exist with the correct contents and that
process.log contains the commands you used.
truth:
Initial file provided before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/data/raw_measurements.csv
Contents (including final newline):
subject_id,temperature,heart_rate
S1,36.6,72
S2,37.1,75
S3,36.9,70
S4,37.3,80
S5,36.8,68

Derived correct artefacts the tests will look for:

1. /home/user/data/summary_stats.txt
   Content:
   Average Temperature: 36.94
   Average Heart Rate: 73
   (with trailing newline after the second line)

2. /home/user/data/raw_measurements_space.txt
   Content:
   subject_id temperature heart_rate
   S1 36.6 72
   S2 37.1 75
   S3 36.9 70
   S4 37.3 80
   S5 36.8 68
   (identical line endings/count to CSV source)

3. /home/user/data/temperature_only.tsv
   Content (TAB shown as \t for clarity):
   subject_id\ttemperature
   S1\t36.6
   S2\t37.1
   S3\t36.9
   S4\t37.3
   S5\t36.8
   (each line terminated by \n)

4. /home/user/data/process.log
   • Must exist.  
   • At least three lines.  
   • Each line must start with “[” followed by a timestamp in
     the format %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S, then “] ”, then the exact command string.  
   • Every line must contain either “awk” or “sed”.  
   • File ends with a newline.

Permissions:
All files in /home/user/data/ are writable by the current user (the container build
will set them to 644 and parent directory to 755).
